description:
You are a system administrator who has been asked to prepare a quick report on application errors that occurred on a single server.  
All raw data is already available on the machine.

Initial state of the machine  
1. A log directory already exists:  
   /home/user/server_logs  
2. Inside that directory there is exactly one file called app.log that contains application log entries.  
3. No other files or directories have been created for you.

Your goal  
Create three new artefacts, all placed in a brand-new directory called  
/home/user/analysis

1. /home/user/analysis/error.log  
   • This file must contain every line from /home/user/server_logs/app.log that contains the exact string “[ERROR]”.  
   • The lines must be written without any modification, in the same order in which they appear in the original file.  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. the very last line is terminated by “\n”).

2. /home/user/analysis/http5xx.log  
   • This file must contain every line from /home/user/server_logs/app.log whose HTTP status code is in the 5xx range (500-599).  
   • The status code is found in the pattern “status=<CODE>”.  
   • Preserve the full original lines and their original order, again ending the file with exactly one trailing newline.

3. /home/user/analysis/error_ips.txt  
   • Extract the client IP address from each line of error.log.  
     The IP address is the value between the first pair of parentheses, e.g. “(192.168.1.11)”.  
   • Produce a list of the **unique** IP addresses, sorted in ascending (lexicographical) order, one IP per line.  
   • The file must end with exactly one trailing newline.

Important verification conditions  
• The directory /home/user/analysis must exist after you finish.  
• The three files listed above must exist and respect the exact content rules (including the single trailing newline rule).  
• Do NOT modify /home/user/server_logs/app.log.  
• Use only tools commonly available on a minimal Debian/Ubuntu installation (grep, awk, sed, sort, uniq, etc. are fine).  
• A correct solution can be accomplished with just 2–3 shell commands, but feel free to structure your work as you prefer.

No other output, files, or directories are required.
truth:
Initial pre-existing file  
Path: /home/user/server_logs/app.log  
Contents (each line ends with “\n”):

2023-04-29 11:22:33 [INFO]  (192.168.1.10) status=200 Request processed successfully
2023-04-29 11:22:34 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:35 [WARN]  (192.168.1.12) status=404 Resource not found
2023-04-29 11:22:36 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=503 Service unavailable
2023-04-29 11:22:37 [INFO]  (192.168.1.10) status=200 Request processed successfully
2023-04-29 11:22:38 [ERROR] (172.16.0.2)   status=501 Not implemented
2023-04-29 11:22:39 [INFO]  (192.168.1.15) status=302 Redirect
2023-04-29 11:22:40 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:41 [INFO]  (192.168.1.10) status=200 Request processed successfully
2023-04-29 11:22:42 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=504 Gateway timeout

Expected artefacts after the agent completes the task

1. /home/user/analysis/error.log  
Exact contents:

2023-04-29 11:22:34 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:36 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=503 Service unavailable
2023-04-29 11:22:38 [ERROR] (172.16.0.2)   status=501 Not implemented
2023-04-29 11:22:40 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:42 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=504 Gateway timeout

(There is a “\n” after the last line.)

2. /home/user/analysis/http5xx.log  
Exact contents:

2023-04-29 11:22:34 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:36 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=503 Service unavailable
2023-04-29 11:22:38 [ERROR] (172.16.0.2)   status=501 Not implemented
2023-04-29 11:22:40 [ERROR] (192.168.1.11) status=500 Internal server error
2023-04-29 11:22:42 [ERROR] (10.0.0.5)     status=504 Gateway timeout

(There is a “\n” after the last line.)

3. /home/user/analysis/error_ips.txt  
Exact contents:

10.0.0.5
172.16.0.2
192.168.1.11

(There is a “\n” after the last line.)

The automated tests will verify:

• That /home/user/analysis exists.  
• That each of the three files exists.  
• That the contents of each file match exactly what is shown above, byte for byte, including the single trailing newline.  
• That /home/user/server_logs/app.log remains unmodified.
